title: GENERAL STANDARD FOR THE USE OF DAIRY TERMS CXS 206-1999 1

sections.section: GENERAL STANDARD FOR THE USE OF DAIRY TERMS CXS 206-1999 1
sections.summary: Title and adoption history for the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999): adopted 1999, amended 2022, and replaces the Code of Principles Concerning Milk and Milk Products.

Summarises the 2022 amendment: textual revisions to Section 5 on labelling, including adding a 'Pre-packaged foods' subsection clarifying labelling per CXS 1-1985 and a new provision that labelling of non‑retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers (CXS 346-2021).

Requires prepackaged milk, milk products and composite milk products to be labelled in accordance with Section 4 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), except where a specific Codex standard or Section 4 of this Standard expressly provides otherwise.

Pre-packaged milk, milk products and composite milk products must be labelled per Section 4 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), unless a specific Codex standard or Section 4 of this Standard provides otherwise.
States that the labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Scope: applies to the use of dairy terms in relation to foods offered to the consumer or for further processing.

Defines key terms for the standard: 'milk' (normal mammary secretion, no additions/extractions), 'milk product', 'composite milk product', 'reconstituted milk product', 'recombined milk product', and 'dairy terms' with specific compositional and processing meanings.
General principle: foods must be described or presented so dairy terms are used correctly, consumers are not misled or confused, and fair practices in the food trade are ensured.

Sets detailed rules on the application of dairy terms: product names must follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods; animal source declarations are required unless omission is not misleading; 'milk' is reserved for foods meeting the definition and may be used for modified or adjusted milks only with declared modifications and, for adjusted milks, subject to national permissions, specified fat/protein limits, permitted methods and declaration; milk-product names may be used only where Codex compositional criteria are met (including for adjusted, reconstituted or recombined materials) and, for composite products, the term 'milk' or a milk-product name may be used if other characterizing ingredients are clearly described; dairy terms are restricted for non-milk foods except where traditional usage or clear descriptive use applies, may be used to describe products containing milk as an essential characterizing ingredient only if not misleading, must not be used for products intended to substitute milk or milk products, and where milk constituents are non-essential they may be limited to the ingredients list.

Requires the food name to be declared according to Section 4.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and that the animal(s) source of the milk be stated adjacent to the product name unless omission would not mislead consumers.

'Milk' may only be used for foods meeting the Section 2.1 definition; raw milk sold as such must be labelled accordingly; milk modified by addition/withdrawal of constituents may still use 'milk' if the modification is clearly described; and milk adjusted for fat/protein for direct consumption may be called 'milk' only where national law permits, specified fat/protein limits are met, permitted methods preserving whey protein-to-casein ratio are used, and the adjustment is declared.

sections.section: 4.3 Use of the names of milk products in Codex commodity standards
sections.summary: Only products complying with a Codex milk-product standard may use the prescribed product name; this applies also to products made from adjusted milk where compositional criteria are met, and modified products may use the milk-product name together with a clear description of the modification if essential characteristics are maintained and compositional modification limits are specified in the relevant standards.

sections.section: ## 4.4 Use of terms for reconstituted and recombined milk products
sections.summary: Reconstituted or recombined milk and milk products may be named as specified in the relevant Codex Standard when produced in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and provided consumers would not be misled.

sections.section: ## 4.5 Use of terms for composite milk products
sections.summary: A composite milk product meeting the Section 2.3 description may be named 'milk' or the relevant milk-product name provided a clear description of other characterizing ingredients appears in close proximity to the name.

sections.section: ## 4.6 Use of dairy terms for other foods
sections.summary: Restricts dairy terms to use for milk, milk products or composite milk products except for traditional or clearly descriptive usages, prohibits labelling or promotion of non-milk products that claim or imply they are milk products, allows dairy terms to describe products where milk constituents are essential and would not mislead, forbids dairy terms for products intended to substitute milk/milk products, and limits use to ingredient lists where milk constituents are non-essential.

Labelling section: repeats that pre-packaged milk, milk products and composite milk products must be labelled per Section 4 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) except where otherwise provided, that non-retail container labelling should follow CXS 346-2021, and notes exclusions for descriptive names and ingredient lists where the consumer would not be misled.

Pre-packaged milk, milk products and composite milk products shall be labelled in accordance with Section 4 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), except as expressly provided otherwise in a specific Codex standard or Section 4 of this Standard.
The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
